title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
text: Generate B alternative realizations Triangle via nonparametric (England-Verrall residual) parametric (Mack normal closed-form) Stage 1 perturbation. output model-agnostic BootstrapTriangle object downstream fit functions (fit_cl / fit_ed / fit_ratio) consume recover parameter process risk decomposition. entry point sits Triangle level – knows nothing CL, ED, SA. fit method later refits model every alt triangle adds Stage 2 process noise using variance recipe. bootstrap object therefore reusable across fit methods. Bootstrap proceeds two conceptual stages (see dev/BOOTSTRAP.md): Stage 1 – parameter uncertainty: residual resample (parametric Normal draw) propagated cumulative loss chain, refitted factors per replicate. produces B alternative mean predictions per cell. Stage 2 – process uncertainty: added inside fit function demand, using method-specific sigma^2. process argument stored metadata consuming fit method knows distribution use.

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
code: bootstrap(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Triangle' bootstrap(   x,   type = c("analytical", "nonparametric", "parametric"),   residual = c("cell", "link"),   hat_adj = TRUE,   demean = TRUE,   process = c("gamma", "od_pois", "normal", "lognormal"),   method = c("ed", "cl", "sa"),   pooling = c("pooled", "separated", "tail_pooled"),   tail = c("auto", "maturity"),   min_pool = 5L,   maturity = NULL,   target = c("loss", "exposure"),   B = 499L,   seed = NULL,   alpha = 1,   quantile_ci = FALSE,   keep_pseudo = FALSE,   ... )

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
text: x Triangle object. ... Reserved future use. type One "analytical", "nonparametric", "parametric". "analytical" draws new link factors N(f_hat, sqrt(Var(f_hat))) (Mack 1993 closed-form propagation; CL ). "nonparametric" resamples standardized residuals reconstructs pseudo triangle (England-Verrall / Pinheiro). "parametric" draws active cell directly ProcessDist(mu_hat, phi) refits synthetic triangle (textbook England-Verrall 1999 parametric bootstrap; supports three methods cl / ed / sa). type left unset, function picks type best matches method: cl defaults "analytical" (fastest), ed / sa default "parametric" (cleanest additive / stage-adaptive variance decomposition). residual Residual scope type = "nonparametric". One "cell" (default – England-Verrall 1999/2002, Pearson residuals incremental cells; ODP GLM equivalent via Renshaw-Verrall 1998) "link" (Mack 1993 / Pinheiro 2003, Pearson residuals link factors). hat_adj Logical. Hat-matrix leverage adjustment (r_h = r / sqrt(1 - h_ii)) cell residual path. Default TRUE (England-Verrall 2002 Addendum); set FALSE use simpler degrees--freedom factor sqrt(n / (n - p)) (England-Verrall 1999). defined residual = "cell"; warned-ignored otherwise. Note hat_adj drops corner cells h_ii = 1 (always (, 1) (1, J) upper triangle); small triangles can meaningful pool reduction – set FALSE matters. demean Logical. Subtract per-group residual mean residual cell-residual pool resampling (de- + mean: remove mean, leaving zero-mean pool). Default TRUE. Shapland (2010, Sec.4.2) discusses one option among others – see Bootstrap SE decomposition vignette trade-. defined residual = "cell"; warned-ignored otherwise. process One "gamma", "od_pois", "normal", "lognormal". Stored metadata; downstream fit functions read choose Stage 2 noise distribution. "gamma" default non-negative right-skewed loss data. "lognormal" reserved Phase 5b.3 currently errors. method Fit-model paradigm whose lower-triangle forward projection bootstrap produce. One "ed" (default – exposure-driven additive recursion across dev; Phase 1 keeps exposure fixed, projected via CL), "cl" (chain-ladder multiplicative recursion across dev), "sa" (stage-adaptive – ED maturity, CL ; currently routes CL kernel pending Phase 4 SA bootstrap). Mirrors method argument fit_loss(); resulting BootstrapTriangle consumed corresponding fit_*(..., bootstrap = bt) branch. "ed" requires residual = "cell"; ED + residual = "link" implemented. pooling Residual-pool grouping. One "pooled", "separated", "tail_pooled". "pooled" shares residuals across links; "separated" keeps development link independent (Mack-faithful); "tail_pooled" uses per-link pools cut single pooled bucket . tail Tail-cut rule pooling = "tail_pooled". One "auto" (cut smallest ata_to whose residual count drops min_pool) "maturity" (cut resolved Maturity change point). min_pool Minimum residual count per per-link pool pooling = "tail_pooled" && tail = "auto". Default 5. maturity Required pooling = "tail_pooled" && tail = "maturity". Four-type dispatch: NULL, Maturity object, string "auto", function function(tri) -> Maturity. target Cumulative metric perturb. One "loss" (default) "exposure". value column $pseudo_triangles named target downstream refit helpers know column read. B Number bootstrap replicates. Default 499 – Davison & Hinkley (1997) convention picks B (B + 1) p integer target quantile p. B = 499 (B + 1 = 500), every one-sided VaR commonly reported reserving – p = 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99 – lands exact integer ordinal index, empirical CI exact without interpolation. Solvency II / K-ICS 99.5% TVaR reporting pass B = 1999; gold-standard published convention pass B = 999. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. alpha Variance exponent Mack's Var(C_{k+1} | C_k) = sigma_k^2 C_k^alpha. Default 1 (volume-weighted). quantile_ci Logical. Opt-flag empirical percentile CI columns (ci_lo / ci_hi = 2.5% / 97.5% quantiles loss_sampled across replicates, Davison & Hinkley (1997) type=1 ordinal) $summary slot. Default FALSE. Normal- approximation CI (mean_proj +/- 1.96 * total_se) derivable total_se usually enough interactive use; set TRUE Solvency II / K-ICS VaR reporting need empirical tail. C kernel computes CI bounds pass SE decomposition (per-cell qsort dominated Stage 1 work), marginal cost FALSE small. keep_pseudo Logical. Whether materialise per-replicate long-format pseudo_triangles slot. Default FALSE (changed TRUE v0.x performance). TRUE builds long-format data.table diagnostic inspection (e.g. raw replicate trajectories, custom quantile work). typical 4-group monthly triangle B = 999 reshape costs ~250-300 ms ~200 MB top $summary; users consume $summary (common case) leave FALSE. fit_ratio() / fit_loss() / fit_exposure() always pass FALSE internally read $summary. Set TRUE explicitly want inspect $pseudo_triangles directly.

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
text: object class BootstrapTriangle (list) elements: pseudo_triangles Long-format data.table columns [groups], cohort, dev, rep, loss. rep ranges 1..B. Observed-region cells contain residual-perturbed (original "analytical") cumulative loss; missing region contains Stage 1 forward projection means. residual_pool data.table standardized residuals used, pool_id column identifying pool residual belongs (depends method/tail). Schema differs residual mode: [groups, cohort, ata_from, ata_to, residual, pool_id] residual = "link", [groups, cohort, dev, residual, pool_id] residual = "cell". f_anchor Per-link Mack factor estimates f_hat n_cohorts. sigma2_anchor Per-link Mack sigma^2 Var(f_hat). meta list(type, residual, hat_adj, process, method, pooling, tail, min_pool, B, seed, alpha, target, groups, maturity).

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(experience) tri <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "surgery"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   exposure = "incr_exposure" )  # Cell-residual bootstrap (default) boots <- bootstrap(tri, type = "nonparametric", residual = "cell",                    hat_adj = TRUE, process = "gamma",                    B = 500, seed = 1) print(boots)  # Link-residual bootstrap (Mack 1993 / Pinheiro 2003 path) boots_link <- bootstrap(tri, type = "nonparametric", residual = "link",                         pooling = "separated", process = "gamma",                         B = 500, seed = 1) } # }

what: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: Identify first dev \(k^{**}\) projected portfolio loss ratio observed stable maximum available development period \(V\). Three complementary stability criteria computed LR backtest path; user selects one defines \(k^{**}\) via method =. Notation mapping (code <-> math): Standard chain-ladder convention: \(\) indexes cohort (origin period), \(k\) indexes development period. maturity point \(k^*\) convergence point \(k^{**}\) live \(k\) axis. Earlier paper drafts used \(v\) (valuation) index Section 11; unify \(k\) consistency. Stability methods (sequence drives pass): "window" Local stability: drift_window[] < max_drift. Fast, misses slow monotone drift fits max_drift per step. "tail" (default, reserving-safe) Global stability: drift_tail[] < max_drift. Catches monotone drift. first passing dev later (conservative) "window". "slope" Trend test: |slope[]| < max_slope. Explicit -trend check; sensitive non-linear trajectories. "" Strictest: three pass simultaneously. four pass vectors (pass_window, pass_tail, pass_slope, pass) underlying diagnostic series returned regardless chosen method, analyst can inspect every criterion re-decide. Across methods, cross-cohort agreement clause dispersion[] < max_dispersion required addition. replaces earlier formulation \(R_k < c \cdot \hat{SE}^{param}_k\) (paper Section 11). paper's SE-normalised form asymptotically broken large portfolios: \(\hat{SE}^{param}\) shrinks \(1/\sqrt{n}\) \(R_k\) structural noise floor, ratio diverges criterion never fires. Caveat (reserving): detected conv_k reflects stability dev_max (\(K_{\max}\)) – asymptotic guarantee projection drift past \(K_{\max}\). Treat conv_k diagnostic ", observe stable", guarantee future stability. reserving applications, prefer method = "tail" "" "window", attach IBNR margin via fit_ratio$summary SE/CI columns, weigh evidence span (dev_max - conv_k): conv_k near dev_max thin evidence.

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
code: detect_convergence(   triangle,   method = c("tail", "window", "slope", "all"),   max_drift = 0.01,   max_slope = 0.001,   max_dispersion = 0.15,   window = 5L,   mat_k = NULL,   holdout_max = NULL,   min_n_cohorts = 5L,   ... )

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: triangle Triangle object (typically as_triangle()). method stability criterion defines conv_k. One "tail" (default), "window", "slope", "". See description semantics reserving caveat. max_drift Upper bound drift metric (window tail), LR units. Default 0.01 (1pp). Raise noisier longer-tail books. max_slope Upper bound |slope[]|, OLS slope \(LR \sim k\) \([k, K_{\max}]\), LR-per-dev units. Default 1e-3 (0.1pp per dev). Used method = "slope" / "". max_dispersion Upper bound cross-cohort dispersion \(\hat{D}_k\). Default 0.15. window Drift window length \(W\) (dev steps): number consecutive valuations used "window" method compute drift_window. Default 5L. Note: functions package also expose window argument (e.g. detect_regime() e-divisive segment width); controls drift metric, e-divisive algorithm. mat_k Pre-computed maturity point. NULL, computed via detect_maturity() applied ratio-based ATA. holdout_max Maximum holdout depth used rolling backtest. NULL, set max(window, floor((dev_max - mat_k) / 2)). min_n_cohorts Minimum number cohorts required compute \(\hat{D}_v\). Default 5L. ... Additional arguments forwarded backtest() (thence fit_ratio()), e.g. loss_method, recent, loss_regime.

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: object class Convergence (named list). Includes slots tabulated notation mapping (dev_max, dev_cand, ratio, revision, drift_window, drift_tail, slope, dispersion), per-method pass vectors (pass_window, pass_tail, pass_slope, pass), threshold parameters, metadata attributes (groups, loss, dispatcher).

what: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: Identify first mature age--age (ata) link Triangle. Internally builds single-variable Link table, computes per-link diagnostic via summary.Link() model = "ata", locates first link whose statistics satisfy maturity criteria. Maturity determined using combination : cv < max_cv rse < max_rse valid_ratio >= min_valid_ratio n_valid >= min_n_valid optional consecutive maturity min_run ata links cv rse must satisfied simultaneously. cv captures raw variability observed ata factors across cohorts, rse reflects precision WLS-estimated factor. Using criteria together provides robust maturity assessment either alone. Default loss = "loss" (cumulative loss). Maturity chain ladder methodologically property loss development: ATA factors cumulative loss stabilize chain ladder becomes reliable, turn makes downstream LR projection reliable. ATA factors ratio (ratio two cumulative quantities) carry additional noise tend give less precise maturity decisions. Override loss specifically want maturity exposure development another cumulative metric.

title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
code: detect_maturity(   x,   loss = "loss",   groups = NULL,   weight = NULL,   alpha = 1,   max_cv = 0.15,   max_rse = 0.05,   min_valid_ratio = 0.5,   min_n_valid = 3L,   min_run = 2L )

title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: x Triangle object. loss Cumulative metric link factor. Default "loss" (chain-ladder convention; see Description). Forwarded as_link(). groups Optional character subset attr(x, "groups") selecting columns define maturity partition. Maturity typically structural property development curve driven coverage rather demographic mix (age, channel, ...), Triangle aggregated c("coverage", "age_band", "channel") may still want per-coverage maturity. NULL (default) keeps current Triangle grouping (fully backward compatible). character(0) pools across groups returns single global maturity row. non-NULL, non-empty value must subset attr(x, "groups"); column order irrelevant. requested groups coarser Triangle grouping, underlying loss / exposure / ratio columns re-aggregated coarser partition computing ata links. weight Optional WLS weight variable. Forwarded as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure underlying WLS fit. Default 1. Forwarded summary.Link(). max_cv Maximum allowed coefficient variation. Default 0.15. max_rse Maximum allowed relative standard error. Default 0.05. min_valid_ratio Minimum proportion finite ata values required. Default 0.5. min_n_valid Minimum number finite ata factors required. Default 3L. min_run Minimum number consecutive ata links satisfying maturity criteria. Default 2L.

title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: data.table class "Maturity" containing one row per group. Columns include ata_from, change (maturity point, .e. -index first mature ata link), ata_link, diagnostic statistics (mean, median, wt, cv, f, f_se, rse, sigma, n_cohorts, n_valid, n_inf, n_nan, valid_ratio). mature link found, values group NA.

what: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: Detect structural change points sequence cohort-level development trajectories. underwriting cohort (indexed cohort "Triangle" object) treated feature vector whose entries selected loss metric observed development periods 1, ..., window. Cohorts ordered underwriting period tested structural shifts multivariate sequence. Multi-group Triangle inputs supported: detection runs independently per group, results combined single Regime object whose $changes, $labels, etc. carry group column. Single-group input retains original scalar / Date-vector / matrix layout backward compatibility. Three detection strategies supported: "e_divisive" Multivariate non-parametric divisive change-point detection via ecp::e.divisive(). number regimes determined data; significant changes sig_level retained. Preferred number regimes known advance. "pelt" Univariate mean change-point detection via changepoint::cpt.mean() PELT algorithm applied first principal component cohort feature matrix. Fast may return multiple changes. "hclust" Ward hierarchical clustering scaled cohort feature matrix, cut n_regimes clusters. Ignores time ordering – useful sanity check since non-adjacent cohorts may cluster together trajectory pattern strictly chronological.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
code: detect_regime(   x,   loss = "ratio",   by = NULL,   window = "auto",   method = c("e_divisive", "pelt", "hclust"),   n_regimes = NULL,   sig_level = 0.05,   min_size = 3L,   treatment = c("latest_only", "segment_wise"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Regime' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Regime' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.Regime' print(x, ...)

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: x object class "Triangle". May contain one groups (per-group detection runs independently). Also used S3 print() method Regime objects. loss Trajectory variable. Default "ratio" (cumulative loss ratio). Accepts column Triangle (e.g. "ratio", "loss", "exposure", "incr_loss", "incr_exposure"), plus three diagnostic derived metrics computed inline per (group, cohort): "loss_ata" Loss age--age factor loss[k+1] / loss[k] – multiplicative loss development speed (CL $f_k$). "exposure_ata" Exposure age--age factor – form exposure. "loss_ed" Loss intensity (loss[k] - loss[k-1]) / exposure[k-1] – additive, exposure-anchored (ED model's $g_k$). "exposure_ed" Alias "exposure_ata" – two differ constant (exposure_ata - 1), PCA standardization detection removes shift, yield identical regime changes. Provided API symmetry loss_ata / loss_ed pair. Derived metrics drop first dev row per cohort (predecessor), re-index dev detection sees contiguous sequence. See vignette("regime") "Choice loss" section guidance loss metric matches suspected event. Grouping column(s) per-combination detection. NULL (default) reuses Triangle's attr(x, "groups") non-empty – detect_regime(tri) dispatches per group automatically – otherwise falls back pooled detection. Pass = character(0) force pooled detection multi-group Triangle, character vector (subset names(x)) dispatch explicit combo, e.g. = "coverage" = c("channel", "coverage"). window Trajectory window. Integer (e.g., 12L) fixed window, string "auto" (default) – resolves group's maturity via detect_maturity(), falling back 6L maturity unavailable (NA, pooled mode, mismatching Triangle's attr("groups")). Cohorts fewer resolved window observed periods dropped. method One "e_divisive", "pelt", "hclust". n_regimes Integer. Number regimes force. NULL means auto-detect "e_divisive" "pelt"; ignored (required equal requested value) "hclust", default 2. sig_level Significance level "e_divisive". Default 0.05. min_size Minimum segment size "e_divisive". Default 3. treatment downstream fits apply Regime $changes contains multiple change points. One : "latest_only" (default) Collapse recent change date drop pre-latest-change cohorts. Single pooled factor estimate surviving (post-latest-change) cohorts. "segment_wise" Preserve change points. segment (consecutive cohorts adjacent changes) gets factor estimate, cohort projected using segment's factor. Recommended multi-regime + long-tail data opt "latest_only" lose self-regime responsiveness older cohorts. ... Reserved future use. object object class "Regime". Used S3 summary() method.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: object class "Regime". single-group input: call Matched call. method Detection method used. loss Trajectory variable used detection. window Trajectory window per combo. Scalar integer single combo analysed; integer vector (one per surviving combo, order $labels / $changes group rows) otherwise. window_mode Either "auto" (resolved per group via detect_maturity()) "manual" (user-supplied integer). cohort Period variable x. labels data.table one row per analysed cohort: [..., cohort, regime, regime_id]. Group columns prepended resolves non-empty vector. changes data.table detected regime changes columns [..., change, regime_id, pre_value, post_value, magnitude]. regime_id = id regime STARTS change (pre-change regime regime_id - 1); matches $labels$regime_id. pre_value / post_value mean loss cohort x dev trajectory windows pre- / post-change regimes; magnitude = |post_value - pre_value|. Empty (zero rows) change detected. n_regimes Number regimes detected. Scalar integer single-combo detection; named integer vector (keyed combo) multi-combo. trajectory Cohort x dev feature matrix used detection. Single matrix single combo; named list matrices multi-combo. pca prcomp object (single combo) named list prcomp objects (multi-combo). dropped Cohorts excluded due window window constraint. Vector (single) / named list (multi). multi_group Logical flag; TRUE detection ran multiple group combos. treatment Either "latest_only" "segment_wise" – value supplied via treatment argument. Read downstream fits (fit_ata(), fit_intensity(), fit_cl(), fit_ed()) decide whether collapse latest change (drop pre-change cohorts, single pooled factor) estimate per-segment factors.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime

what: Apply the ED additive variance recursion to a CL worker's $full — .apply_ed_variance
title: Apply the ED additive variance recursion to a CL worker's $full — .apply_ed_variance
text: Point projection (loss_proj) preserved – identical CL self-weighted ED. variance accumulation differs: CL recursion: proc_{k+1} = f^2 * proc_k + sigma^2 * C_k (multiplicative scaling – prior variance amplified f^2 step). ED recursion: proc_{k+1} = proc_k + sigma^2 * C_k (additive – prior variance carried forward unchanged). share per-link sigma^2 f_var (= Var(f_hat_k)) estimates. recursion per (group, cohort).

what: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor
title: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor
text: Internal helper used amount_divisor = "auto". Considers SI/financial prefix set {1, 1e3, 1e6, 1e9, 1e12} (unit / thousand / million / billion / trillion) picks largest divisor median / divisor still rounds (%.1f) non-zero label. 0.05 threshold label format "0.0" precision wiped , divisors disqualified. Falls back 1 data finite positive values. "largest divisor still rounds non-zero" rule favours labels 0.X range (3 chars) X.X / XX.X / XXX.X (3-5 chars). keeps -cell heatmap text compact; colour fill carries precise value. Examples (median -> divisor, label): 5e2  -> 1e3,  "0.5" 5e7  -> 1e9,  "0.1" (since 5e7 / 1e9 = 0.05 >= 0.05) 5e10 -> 1e12, "0.1" 5e12 -> 1e12, "5.0" (capped largest candidate) numeric >= 0.05 test deliberate: Windows R's sprintf("%.1f", 0.05) rounds "0.0" Linux / macOS round "0.1". Comparing numerically (tiny 1e-10 slack) produces deterministic, platform-independent choice.

title: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor

what: Per-replicate BF / Cape Cod composition from two BootstrapTriangle — .bf_compose_bootstrap
title: Per-replicate BF / Cape Cod composition from two BootstrapTriangle — .bf_compose_bootstrap
text: Given paired loss-side exposure-side BootstrapTriangle objects (keep_pseudo = TRUE per-replicate cohort--dev cum loss / cum exposure means available), compose BF / Cape Cod ultimate distribution per replicate: replicate \(b\), derive \(q_i^b =     L_{obs,} / L_{ult,}^{CL,b}\) loss-side Stage 1 mean trajectory (last-dev cell). Derive \(E_i^{ult,b}\) exposure-side Stage 1 mean last-dev cell. BF: \(L_{ult,}^{b} = L_{obs,} +     (1 - q_i^b) \cdot \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E_i^{ult,b}\). Cape Cod: per group \(\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC,b} =     \sum_i L_{obs,} / \sum_i E_i^{ult,b} \cdot q_i^b\), plug BF formula. Cell-level projection per replicate: scale per-replicate CL emergence pattern land \(L_{ult,}^{b}\) last dev. Cell-level cohort-level SE / CI SD / quantiles across replicates.

title: Loss ratio SE: ratio_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_ratio_se
text: Ratio-specific internal helper. Two variants: "fixed" (default) Premium treated fixed (non-random). \(\mathrm{SE}(L/P) = \mathrm{SE}(L) / P\). Strictly degenerate case delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0. "delta" First-order Taylor (delta method) including exposure uncertainty loss-exposure correlation rho: \(\mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx (\mathrm{SE}(L)/P)^2 +       (L \cdot \mathrm{SE}(P) / P^2)^2 -       2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P) / P^3\). variance clipped zero square root (high rho can drive linearised estimate negative). exported; called fit_ratio(). "fixed" branch encodes actuarial assumption earned premium known (estimated), helper generic ratio-SE utility.

what: Compute ED intensity variance for each development link — .ed_g_var
title: Compute ED intensity variance for each development link — .ed_g_var
text: Internal helper computing \(\mathrm{Var}(\hat{g}_k) = \sigma^2_k / W_k\) \(W_k = \sum_i (C^P_{,k})^{2 - \alpha}\). Buehlmann-Straub (1970) volume-weighted variance applied ED intensity \(g_k = \sum_i \Delta L_{,k} / \sum_i P_{,k-1}\). Paradigm pairing: package keeps two natural analytical variance helpers, one per paradigm-target pair: .mack_f_var() (CL / Mack 1993 applied f-factor) .ed_g_var() (ED / Buehlmann-Straub 1970 applied g-intensity). cross-paradigm pairs (.mack_g_var, .bs_f_var) algebraically derivable via \(g_k = f_k - 1\) (therefore \(\sigma^2_g = \sigma^2_f\)), intentionally provided separate functions avoid suggesting paradigm mismatch encouraged user code. Conceptually factor-level helper (operates per-link $link $selected slots), parallel .mack_f_var(ata_fit: ATAFit). Accepts either "IntensityFit" (factor-level diagnostic ED, sibling "ATAFit") "EDFit" (projection-level, exposes factor-level slots superset). "IntensityFit" path conceptually clean entry point factor-level callers; "EDFit" accepted projection-level callers already hold fit object. Used fit_ed() method = "mack" fit_ratio() ED component.

title: Compute ED intensity variance for each development link — .ed_g_var
code: .ed_g_var(x, alpha = 1)

title: Compute ED intensity variance for each development link — .ed_g_var

title: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per development link: $$\Delta C^L_{,k+1} = g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(1 / (C^P_{,k})^{2 - \alpha}\), corresponding variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(\Delta C^L_{,k+1}) \propto (C^P_{,k})^{\alpha}\).

title: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed

what: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per age--age link: $$C_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot C_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(w_{,k} / C_{,k}^{2 - \alpha}\), \(w_{,k}\) either constant column supplied via weights. corresponds Mack's variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(C_{,k+1} \mid C_{,k}) \propto C_{,k}^{\alpha}\). one observation available link, factor computed directly loss_to / loss_from standard errors set NA. Near-zero values f_se sigma (tol) set zero avoid numerical noise essentially perfect fits.

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
code: .lm_link(x, weights = 1, alpha = 1, na_rm = TRUE, tol = 1e-12)

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
text: x object class "Link". weights Either length-one numeric scalar (default 1) single column name present Link data provides per-row weights. alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure. Default 1. na_rm Logical; TRUE (default), rows non-finite non-positive loss_from dropped fitting. Note loss_to = 0 permitted, zero cumulative values valid observations (e.g. claims yet developed early development periods). tol Non-negative numeric scalar. Values tol set zero. Default 1e-12.

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link

what: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
title: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
text: Internal helper computing: $$\mathrm{Var}(\hat{f}_k) = \frac{\sigma^2_k}{W_k}$$ \(W_k = \sum_i w_{,k} \cdot C_{,k}^\alpha\). consistent WLS weight \(w_{,k} / C_{,k}^{2-\alpha}\) used .lm_ata() follows Mack (1993)'s distribution-free standard-error derivation chain ladder reserve estimator. Paradigm pairing: package keeps two natural analytical variance helpers, one per paradigm-target pair: .mack_f_var() (CL / Mack 1993 applied f-factor) .ed_g_var() (ED / Buehlmann-Straub 1970 applied g-intensity). share underlying volume-weighted variance idea (\(\sigma^2_g = \sigma^2_f\) via \(g_k = f_k - 1\)), operate different Link columns (f reads loss_to/loss_from; g reads loss_delta/exposure_from) produce differently-named output columns (f_var / g_var), kept separate helpers. Also used fit_ratio() CL component.

title: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
code: .mack_f_var(ata_fit, alpha = 1)

title: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var

text: Four-type dispatch mirroring .resolve_bootstrap() returning pair BootstrapTriangle objects (loss + exposure) – BF / Cape Cod compose loss-side parameter uncertainty (via \(q_i^b\)) exposure-side parameter uncertainty (via \(E_i^{ult,b}\)) single ultimate distribution. what: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: Compute group-wise summary statistics age--age factors object class "Link". helper backs summary.Link() dispatcher model = "ata". serves two purposes: Diagnostics: provides descriptive statistics (mean, median, wt, cv) help user assess stability consistency observed ata factors across cohorts. Estimation: fits -intercept weighted least squares model per ata link produce WLS-estimated factor (f), standard error (f_se), relative standard error (rse), Mack sigma (sigma). used downstream detect_maturity() fit_ata().

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
code: .summarize_link_ata(object, alpha = 1, digits = 3, ...)

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: object object class "Link", typically produced as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure WLS fit. Default 1. digits Number decimal places round numeric columns. Default 3. Pass NULL skip rounding. ... Additional arguments passed internal WLS estimation.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: data.table class "ATASummary" containing one row per ata link: ata_from, ata_to, ata_link Link identifiers. mean Arithmetic mean observed ata factors. median Median observed ata factors. wt Volume-weighted mean: \(\sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\), independent alpha. cv Coefficient variation observed ata factors (\(SD / mean\)). Used detect_maturity() assess stability. f WLS-estimated factor. Equals wt alpha = 2 zero loss_from rows present. f_se Standard error WLS-estimated factor. rse Relative standard error WLS-estimated factor (\(f\_se / f\)). sigma Mack sigma (residual standard deviation WLS fit). Used Mack variance estimation. n_cohorts Total number observations link. n_valid Number finite ata values. n_inf Number infinite ata values. n_nan Number NaN ata values. valid_ratio Proportion finite ata values (\(n\_valid / n\_cohorts\)).

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: wt f weighted averages observed ata factors, differ weights assigned observations included: wt Volume-weighted mean: \(wt = \sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\). Computed rows loss_from loss_to finite, including rows either value zero. Independent alpha. f WLS-estimated factor. rows loss_from > 0 used, since loss_from = 0 causes numerical issues WLS weights (\(w = value\_from^{\alpha}\)). alpha = 2, f wt numerically equivalent (assuming zero loss_from rows). alpha \ne 2, diverge. Therefore wt f can differ two reasons: Zero exclusion: rows loss_from = 0 included wt excluded f. typically affects early development periods cohorts yet accumulated claims. Alpha effect: alpha \ne 2, WLS weights differ volume weights used wt, leading different estimates. Comparing wt f can help diagnose whether choice alpha materially affects estimated factor.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: input "Link" object contains weight column (added as_link() weight supplied), column automatically used WLS weight place loss_from. useful loss = "ratio", loss_from carries exposure information external exposure variable exposure used instead.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: coefficient variation defined : $$cv = \frac{SD(f_k)}{\bar{f}_k}$$ \(f_k\) individual observed ata values link \(k\) \(\bar{f}_k\) arithmetic mean. cv reflects relative spread observed factors across cohorts, regardless exposure scale. used detect_maturity() one criteria determining maturity point.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: relative standard error defined : $$rse = \frac{SE(\hat{f}_k)}{\hat{f}_k}$$ \(SE(\hat{f}_k)\) standard error WLS-estimated factor. Unlike cv, treats cohorts equally, rse gives weight cohorts larger exposures (via WLS weights). small rse indicates WLS estimate precise, tends occur : (1) many cohorts, (2) exposures large, (3) observed ata values consistent across cohorts.

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
text: Internal helper computes group-wise summary statistics incremental loss intensity \(g\) dual-variable Link object (built exposure set). Dispatched via summary.Link() model = "ed". Two purposes: Diagnostics: provides descriptive statistics (mean, median, wt, cv) help user assess stability consistency observed \(g\) values across cohorts. Estimation: fits -intercept weighted least squares model per development link produce WLS-estimated intensity (g), standard error (g_se), relative standard error (rse), residual sigma (sigma). used downstream fit_ed().

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
code: .summarize_link_ed(object, alpha = 1, digits = 5, ...)

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
text: object Link object built exposure set, typically produced as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure WLS fit. Default 1. digits Number decimal places round numeric columns. Default 5. Pass NULL skip rounding. ... Additional arguments passed internal WLS estimation.

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
text: data.table class "EDSummary" containing one row per development link descriptive statistics WLS estimates.

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
text: wt g weighted averages observed intensities, differ weights assigned: wt Exposure-weighted mean: \(wt = \sum \Delta C^L_{,k+1} / \sum C^P_{,k}\). Computed rows values finite. Independent alpha. g WLS-estimated intensity lm(loss_delta ~ exposure_from + 0). rows exposure_from > 0 used. alpha = 2, g wt numerically equivalent.

title: Unified theme dispatcher (internal) — .switch_theme
text: Produces ggplot2::theme() configured one three usage contexts: "view": RStudio screen exploration; sizes inherit ggplot2 defaults. "save": embedding spreadsheet tools Excel; fixed axis / title sizes, plot background untouched. "shiny": embedding Shiny apps; "save" plus configurable transparent plot background. Setting *.size argument 0 replaces element ggplot2::element_blank().

title: Unified theme dispatcher (internal) — .switch_theme
code: .switch_theme(   theme = c("view", "save", "shiny"),   family = getOption("lossratio.font"),   x.size = NULL,   y.size = NULL,   t.size = NULL,   s.size = NULL,   l.size = NULL,   x.face = "plain",   y.face = "plain",   t.face = "plain",   s.face = "plain",   l.face = "plain",   x.angle = 0,   y.angle = 0,   x.hjust = 0.5,   x.vjust = 0.5,   y.hjust = NULL,   y.vjust = NULL,   show_grid_major = FALSE,   show_grid_minor = FALSE,   legend.key.height = NULL,   legend.key.width = NULL,   legend.position = "right",   legend.justification = "center",   plot.background.fill = "transparent" )

NULL leaves ggplot default; 0 hides element. x.face, y.face, t.face, s.face, l.face Font faces ("plain", "bold", "italic", "bold.italic"). x.angle, y.angle, x.hjust, x.vjust, y.hjust, y.vjust Axis text placement. show_grid_major, show_grid_minor Whether draw grid lines. legend.key.height, legend.key.width, legend.position, legend.justification Legend geometry. plot.background.fill Fill plot panel background; used theme = "shiny".

title: Sample loss experience data — experience
text: Synthetic long-format loss experience data demonstrating lossratio package workflow. Generated deterministically data-raw/make_experience.R (set.seed = 20260501). Per-coverage calibration scalars (target ultimate LR, cohort exposure volume, cell-level noise CV) measured real long-term Korean health-insurance portfolio baked constants – real-portfolio data shipped. runoff shape uses small dev-1 "waiting-period" dip followed roughly constant incremental loss per dev, cumulative LR rises monotonically toward target. surgery coverage carries synthetic 2024-07 cohort regime change (target LR scaled 0.6) detect_regime() example clear shift find.

title: Fit age-to-age development factors — fit_ata
text: Estimate age--age (ata) development factors object class "Link" return unified "ATAFit" object bundles: Summary statistics WLS estimates (summary) summary.Link() model = "ata". Selected factors (selected) ready chain ladder projection, optional maturity filtering LOCF fill. Maturity diagnostics (maturity) detect_maturity().

title: Fit age-to-age development factors — fit_ata
code: fit_ata(   x,   loss = "loss",   weight = NULL,   alpha = 1,   na_method = c("locf", "none"),   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none"),   recent = NULL,   regime = NULL,   maturity = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit age-to-age development factors — fit_ata
text: x object class "Link", typically produced as_link(). loss Cumulative metric link factor. Default "loss". Forwarded as_link(). weight Optional WLS weight variable. Forwarded as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure. Default 1. na_method Method used fill NA values f_sel. One "locf" (default) "none". Passed .filter_ata(). sigma_method Method used extrapolate sigma links estimated. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none". "mack" applies Mack (1993, Appendix B) tail estimator last unestimated link , falling back LOCF earlier ones warning. "none" performs extrapolation; sigma stays NA downstream variance terms drop links via finite-value guards. Passed .extrapolate_sigma_ata(). recent Optional positive integer. supplied, recent recent periods Link triangle used factor estimation. Applied maturity filtering. Default NULL (use periods). regime Optional regime specification cohort cutoff. Accepts: NULL (default – filter), Regime object (detect_regime() regime_at()), string "auto" (internal detect_regime(tri, loss = "ratio") call), function function(tri) -> Regime deferred custom-config detection. supplied, cohorts strictly resolved change date excluded estimation. maturity Optional maturity specification filtering ata links. Accepts four input types: NULL (default) maturity filter. Maturity object Use -. Typically built via detect_maturity() maturity_at(). "auto" Detect maturity internally via detect_maturity(x) input triangle. title: Bornhuetter-Ferguson projection — fit_bf
text: Fit Bornhuetter-Ferguson (1972) projection "Triangle" object. BF estimator blends observed cumulative loss cohort priori expected loss ratio (ELR) applied cohort's ultimate exposure, weighted expected unemerged fraction \(1 - q_i\): $$\hat L_{ult, }^{BF} = L_{obs, } +   (1 - q_i) \cdot \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E_i^{ult}$$ \(L_{obs, }\): cohort \(\)'s observed cumulative loss latest observed development period. \(q_i = L_{obs, } / \hat L_{ult, }^{CL}\): expected emerged fraction, equivalent inverse cumulative loss development factor (LDF) cohort \(\). \(\mathrm{ELR}_i\): user-supplied priori expected loss ratio cohort \(\) (prior argument). \(E_i^{ult}\): cohort \(\)'s ultimate exposure, projected via chain ladder exposure column. peer worker alongside fit_cl() / fit_ed() / fit_loss(). Standalone BF recipe – composition fit_ratio() part worker. Point projection always computed; bootstrap SE / CI opt-via bootstrap = TRUE (Phase 3b). Closed-form Mack (2008) MSEP yet implemented.

Must cover every cohort present x (extras silently dropped, missing cohorts raise error). per-group data.frame (grouping columns + elr) One ELR per group, broadcast every cohort group. Useful single priori ELR set per line business. Must cover every group present x. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration. Five forms accepted: NULL / FALSE (default) Point estimate – bootstrap SE/CI. TRUE / "auto" Internal bootstrap() calls (one loss, one exposure) sharing seed replicate indices align across two simulations. Named list list(loss = BootstrapTriangle, exposure = BootstrapTriangle) Pre-built objects bootstrap(). Must matching meta$B / meta$seed per-replicate composition well-defined; meta$target must "loss" "exposure" respectively. Function function(tri) -> list(loss = ..., exposure = ...) Lazy spec invoked input Triangle (leakage-safe backtest()). Latest observed cumulative loss perturbed BF recipe – treated cohort anchor, mirroring point-estimate formula. B Integer number bootstrap replicates. Used bootstrap resolves "auto". Default 999. seed Optional integer seed reproducible bootstrap. Default NULL. type One "parametric" (default), "nonparametric", "analytical". latter reserved Phase 3c (Mack 2008 closed-form MSEP) currently errors. residual Residual scope type = "nonparametric". One "cell" (default) "link". See bootstrap(). process One "gamma" (default), "od_pois", "normal". See bootstrap(). alpha Numeric scalar passed inner fit_cl() fit_exposure() calls. Default 1. sigma_method Sigma extrapolation method forwarded fit_cl() / fit_exposure(). Default "locf". recent Optional positive integer; calendar-diagonal filter forwarded inner fits. Default NULL. regime Optional regime specification forwarded inner loss exposure fits. See fit_cl() four-type dispatch. maturity Optional maturity specification forwarded inner loss fit. See fit_cl() four-type dispatch. conf_level Confidence level bootstrap quantile CI loss_ult. Default 0.95. ... Reserved future extension (currently unused).

title: Cape Cod projection (Stanard 1985) — fit_cc
text: Fit Cape Cod projection "Triangle" object. Cape Cod prior-free Bornhuetter-Ferguson variant introduced Stanard (1985): priori expected loss ratio estimated data portfolio-pooled quantity, plugged BF formula. $$\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC} =   \frac{\sum_i L_{obs, }}{\sum_i E_i^{ult} \cdot q_i}$$ \(L_{obs, }\): cohort \(\)'s observed cumulative loss latest observed development period. \(q_i = L_{obs, } / \hat L_{ult, }^{CL}\): expected emerged fraction (inverse cumulative LDF). \(E_i^{ult}\): cohort \(\)'s ultimate exposure (projected via chain ladder exposure). Given \(\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC}\), per-cohort ultimate obtained BF formula single pooled ELR: $$\hat L_{ult, }^{CC} = L_{obs, } +   (1 - q_i) \cdot \widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC} \cdot E_i^{ult}$$ multiple groups present, \(\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC}\) computed within group (pooled across groups) group retains portfolio-level ELR estimate. peer worker alongside fit_bf() / fit_cl() / fit_ed(). Standalone Cape Cod recipe – composition fit_ratio() part worker. Point projection always computed; bootstrap SE / CI opt-via bootstrap = TRUE (Phase 3b). bootstrap path also produces per-replicate pooled ELR draws (elr_cc_se, elr_cc_cv, elr_cc_ci_lo, elr_cc_ci_hi) since Cape Cod ELR data-driven thus uncertain.

what: Fit a chain ladder projection on the exposure triangle — fit_exposure
title: Fit a chain ladder projection on the exposure triangle — fit_exposure
text: Project cumulative exposure across cohort x development grid chain ladder estimator. Two variance recursions supported: "ed" (default) Additive recursion. Empirically robust long-projection exposure triangles – multiplicative scaling classical CL recursion can blow cohort-wise heterogeneity (regime changes premium, channel changes, amendments). See dev/exposure_projection.qmd. "cl" Mack (1993) multiplicative recursion. Point projection identical ED; SE accumulation differs. methods share point estimate – self-weighted ED exposure mathematically equivalent chain ladder column (f_k = 1 + g_k). operational difference cumulative variance propagated forward.

text: Unlike ATA factors, CV / RSE drive detect_maturity() threshold, ED intensities behave differently – \(g_k \0\) late development CV / RSE blow construction, instability. fit_intensity() therefore deliberately omits maturity parameter, detect_maturity() rejects IntensityFit input informative error.

title: Fit a loss projection on a Triangle — fit_loss
text: Project cumulative loss across cohort x development grid. fit_loss() role-specific dispatcher loss side – forwards worker selected method: "ed" (default) fit_ed() – pure exposure-driven (additive). Unconditional safe baseline; maturity dependency. "cl" fit_cl() – pure Mack chain ladder (multiplicative). Classical reference. "sa" fit_sa() – stage-adaptive composition: ED maturity point, CL . "bf" fit_bf() – Bornhuetter-Ferguson; requires prior ELR (scalar per-cohort table) passed via .... "cc" fit_cc() – Cape Cod (BF pooled ELR derived data). dispatcher returns LossFit object whose $full schema uniform across methods (loss_obs, loss_proj, loss_total_se, loss_ci_lo, loss_ci_hi, exposure_obs, exposure_proj, incr_exposure_proj, plus method-specific extras). Missing slots worker outputs (e.g. loss_ata_fit ED, ed/selected CL/BF/CC) synthesized NULL downstream code fit_ratio() can guard uniformly.

"mack" applies Mack (1993, Appendix B) tail estimator last unestimated link , falling back LOCF earlier ones warning. "none" performs extrapolation; sigma stays NA downstream variance terms drop links via finite-value guards. Passed .extrapolate_sigma_ata(). recent Optional positive integer; calendar-diagonal filter. maturity Optional maturity specification. Accepts standard 4-type dispatch (NULL / Maturity / "auto" / function). used "cl", "sa", "bf". Default "auto". tail Tail factor (logical numeric). Forwarded "cl" / "sa" workers. Default FALSE. conf_level Confidence level analytical CI loss projection (loss_ci_lo, loss_ci_hi). Default 0.95. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration. Five forms accepted (see fit_sa() / fit_ed() / fit_cl() method-specific defaults). B Integer number bootstrap replicates. Default 999. seed Optional integer seed. type Bootstrap process type. Forwarded applicable ("sa", "bf", "cc"). ... Method-specific arguments forwarded chosen worker. method = "bf", prior required.

what: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
text: Unified interface loss ratio projection "Triangle" object. Three projection methods available: "ed" (default) Exposure-driven development periods. future increments \(g_k \cdot C^P_k\). Unconditional safe baseline – maturity dependency, robust early-dev ATA volatility. "cl" Chain ladder development periods. Equivalent classical Mack (1993) recursion. "sa" Stage-adaptive composition: ED maturity, CL maturity. Requires maturity detection (2-pass), uses age-- age factors stabilised. maturity: age--age factors volatile, exposure-driven projection \(\Delta C^L = g_k \cdot C^P_k\) anchors estimate exposure volume. maturity: age--age factors stable, chain ladder projection \(C^L_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C^L_k\) preserves cohort's observed level. cases, exposure projected forward using chain ladder: $$\hat{C}^P_{,k+1} = f^P_k \cdot \hat{C}^P_{,k}$$ function composition layer fit_loss() fit_exposure(): delegates loss projection fit_loss(), retrieves embedded ExposureFit, composes loss-ratio point + variance via delta method (se_method = "fixed" "delta"). See ARCHITECTURE.md layered design.

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
code: fit_ratio(   x,   method = c("ed", "cl", "sa"),   loss_alpha = 1,   loss_regime = NULL,   exposure_method = c("cl", "ed"),   exposure_alpha = 1,   exposure_regime = NULL,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none"),   recent = NULL,   maturity = "auto",   se_method = c("fixed", "delta"),   rho = 0.95,   conf_level = 0.95,   bootstrap = NULL,   B = 999L,   seed = NULL )

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
text: x object class "Triangle". standardized "loss" "exposure" columns used (as_triangle() produces ). method One "ed" (default), "cl", "sa". loss_alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure loss estimation. Default 1. loss_regime Optional regime specification loss-side filter. Accepts four input types: NULL (default) regime filter. Regime object Use -. Typically built via detect_regime() regime_at(). "auto" Detect regime internally via detect_regime(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Regime object (NULL). Behavior depends method: "sa" Hybrid filter. Pre-change cohorts dropped development periods maturity point (ED phase); post-maturity (CL) cells use recent-diagonal window across cohorts. preserves CL stability protecting ED intensities regime change. "ed", "cl" Simple cohort cut: cohorts strictly change date excluded estimation. exposure_method One "cl" (default) "ed". Forwarded fit_exposure() constructing exposure projection. exposure_alpha Numeric scalar exposure chain ladder. Default 1. exposure_regime Exposure-side regime specification. four input types loss_regime (NULL / Regime / "auto" / function). Default NULL – exposure fit full triangle independently loss_regime (lazy default). Set explicitly regime shift affects exposure accrual . sigma_method Method used extrapolate sigma links estimated. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none". "mack" applies Mack (1993, Appendix B) tail estimator last unestimated link , falling back LOCF earlier ones warning. "none" performs extrapolation; sigma stays NA downstream variance terms drop links via finite-value guards. Passed .extrapolate_sigma_ata(). recent Optional positive integer estimation window. Default NULL. maturity Optional maturity specification. Accepts four input types: NULL maturity filter. Disables SA-mode switch detection. Maturity object Use -. Typically built via detect_maturity() maturity_at(). "auto" (default) Detect maturity internally via detect_maturity(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Maturity object (e.g. maturity_spec()) deferred custom-config detection. method = "sa", also determines switch point ED CL phases. se_method Method computing ratio_se = SE(L/P). One : "fixed" (default) Premium treated fixed (non-random). \(\mathrm{SE}(L/P) = \mathrm{SE}(L) / P\). Strictly, delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0, .e., degenerate case assumption exposure known. "delta" Full delta method including exposure uncertainty loss-exposure correlation rho: $$\mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{P^2}         + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(P)}{P^4}         - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P)}{P^3}$$ rho Numeric scalar (-1, 1); assumed correlation ultimate loss ultimate exposure. used se_method = "delta". Default 0.95, matching strong positive correlation typically observed cumulative loss cumulative exposure long-tail health portfolios (analogous paid/incurred correlation used Munich chain ladder). conf_level Confidence level used ratio_ci_lo/ratio_ci_hi cohort summary. Default 0.95. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration. Five forms accepted: NULL (default) Auto-resolved method: bootstrap "sa"/"ed", analytical "cl". Matches legacy behavior. TRUE / FALSE Back-compat legacy logical arg. TRUE triggers "auto"; FALSE disables. "auto" Internal bootstrap() call loss triangle defaults (type = "analytical", process = "normal", target = "loss"). BootstrapTriangle Pre-built object bootstrap(). Must meta$target == "loss". Function function(tri) -> BootstrapTriangle Lazy spec invoked input Triangle (leakage-safe backtest()). Premium held observed values bootstrap (loss-convention). ratio_se recomputed bootstrap-derived loss_total_se via .compute_ratio_se(), combined exposure-side SE per se_method ("fixed" ignores exposure SE; "delta" uses exposure_total_se inner fit_exposure() plus rho correlation). B Integer number bootstrap replications. Used bootstrap resolves "auto". Default 999. seed Optional integer seed reproducible bootstrap. Default NULL.

what: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
title: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
text: Project cumulative loss across cohort x development grid using stage-adaptive (SA) method: ED maturity point, CL . SA composes projection paradigms anchored per-group maturity switch – 2-pass fit (maturity detection via fit_ata(), SA projection ). SA worker – standalone, internal method dispatch. role-specific entry point fit_loss() dispatches method = "sa" function; users can also call fit_sa() directly.

title: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
code: fit_sa(   x,   loss = "loss",   exposure = "exposure",   alpha = 1,   exposure_fit = NULL,   exposure_method = c("cl", "ed"),   exposure_alpha = 1,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none"),   recent = NULL,   regime = NULL,   maturity = "auto",   tail = FALSE,   conf_level = 0.95,   bootstrap = NULL,   B = 999L,   seed = NULL,   type = c("parametric", "nonparametric", "analytical") )

title: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
text: x "Triangle" object. standardized "loss" "exposure" columns used (as_triangle() produces ). loss Cumulative loss column name. Default "loss". exposure Cumulative exposure column name. Default "exposure". alpha Variance-structure exponent loss fit. Default 1. exposure_fit Optional pre-built ExposureFit supplying exposure projection. NULL, fit_sa() calls fit_exposure() internally using exposure_method, exposure_alpha, resolved regime. exposure_method One "cl" (default) "ed". Used exposure_fit = NULL. exposure_alpha Variance-structure exponent exposure fit. Default 1. sigma_method Method used extrapolate sigma links estimated. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none". "mack" applies Mack (1993, Appendix B) tail estimator last unestimated link , falling back LOCF earlier ones warning. "none" performs extrapolation; sigma stays NA downstream variance terms drop links via finite-value guards. Passed .extrapolate_sigma_ata(). recent Optional positive integer; calendar-diagonal filter. regime Optional regime specification (loss-side). Accepts standard 4-type dispatch (NULL / Regime / "auto" / function). SA mode resolved regime drives hybrid 2-pass filter (cohort cut ED phase, calendar-diagonal wedge CL phase). maturity Maturity specification. Default "auto". Accepts standard 4-type dispatch (NULL / Maturity / "auto" / function). SA requires maturity – NULL disables SA entirely (use ED CL directly case). tail Logical numeric; tail factor CL phase. Forwarded internal exposure fit relevant. conf_level Confidence level analytical CI loss projection. Default 0.95. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration (NULL / TRUE / FALSE / "auto" / BootstrapTriangle / lazy function). Default NULL resolves "auto" (residual bootstrap) SA. B Integer number bootstrap replicates. Default 999. seed Optional integer seed. type Bootstrap process type. Default "parametric". (used bootstrap = "auto".)

what: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: Captures detect_maturity() arguments without running detection. Returns closure consumer (fit_* backtest()) invokes internal triangle. point conditional / deferred detection – value $k^*$ depends cells caller decides expose: fit_ratio() / fit_loss(), spec invoked full triangle user passed . backtest(), spec invoked masked triangle holdout fold, never full triangle. Held-diagonals removed detect_maturity() sees data, detected $k^*$ depends cells masked fit can also see. leakage-safe contract maturity_spec(). Contrast maturity_at(), produces eager "Maturity" object whose value fixed construction time (independent fold's masked data). Use maturity_spec() want $k^*$ re-detected per fold backtest honestly answers "given data available fold, picked?" Use maturity_at() want fixed value tested across folds.

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
code: maturity_spec(...)

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: ... kwargs passed verbatim detect_maturity() spec invoked (e.g. loss, groups, min_run, max_cv, max_rse, min_valid_ratio, min_n_valid).

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: function one argument ("Triangle") returning "Maturity" object. caller decides triangle pass (full vs. masked); inside backtest() always masked training triangle.

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Capture detection arguments, defer execution until fit time. spec <- maturity_spec(min_run = 2, max_cv = 0.04)  # In fit_ratio(): closure is invoked on the user's `tri`. fit <- fit_ratio(tri, maturity = maturity_spec(min_run = 2))  # In backtest(): closure is invoked on the *masked* triangle of # each holdout fold, so detected k* never peeks at held-out cells. bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L,                maturity = maturity_spec(min_run = 2, max_cv = 0.04)) } # }

what: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: User-facing helper hand-specifying regime change (set per-group changes) without running detect_regime(). returned "Regime" object plugs function consumes Regime – fit_ratio(), fit_loss(), fit_exposure(), backtest(), regime-change resolver – carrying $changes schema detect_regime() output. Argument syntax mirrors data.frame() / data.table(): named vectors equal length, one must change. named arguments treated group columns.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
code: regime_at(..., treatment = c("latest_only", "segment_wise"))

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: ... Named vectors equal length. Must include change (coercible Date; start--regime date post-change regime). named arguments interpreted group column values (e.g. coverage, channel). group columns result pooled (single-row) Regime. treatment downstream fits apply Regime $changes contains multiple change points. "latest_only" (default) collapses recent change drops pre-latest cohorts (single pooled factor). "segment_wise" preserves changes estimates one factor per segment (cohort projected segment's factor). See detect_regime() full semantics.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: object class "Regime" minimal schema needed downstream consumers: method "manual". loss NA_character_ (detection metric). changes data.table columns [<group cols>..., change, regime_id, pre_value, post_value, magnitude]. regime_id 2L (post-change regime) row; stats columns NA_real_. groups Character vector group column names (possibly empty). multi_group TRUE group columns one unique group row. Detection-specific slots (labels, trajectory, pca, dropped, n_regimes, window, window_mode, pca) left empty / NA object can still printed consumed clearly distinguishable detected Regime.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pooled change (no group columns) regime_at(change = "2024-07-01")  # Single-group change regime_at(coverage = "surgery", change = "2024-04-01")  # Multiple groups, one column regime_at(coverage = c("surgery", "cancer"),           change   = c("2024-04-01", "2023-09-01"))  # Multi-dimensional group keys regime_at(coverage = c("surgery", "surgery"),           channel  = c("online", "agent"),           change   = c("2024-04-01", "2024-05-01")) } # }

what: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: Captures detect_regime() arguments without running detection. Returns closure consumer (fit_* backtest()) invokes internal triangle. point conditional / deferred detection – change points depend cells caller decides expose: fit_ratio() / fit_loss() / fit_exposure(), spec invoked full triangle user passed . backtest(), spec invoked masked triangle holdout fold, never full triangle. Held-diagonals removed detect_regime() sees data, detected change points depend cells masked fit can also see. leakage-safe contract regime_spec(). Contrast regime_at(), produces eager "Regime" object whose change points fixed construction time (independent fold's masked data). Use regime_spec() want change points re-detected per fold backtest honestly answers "given data available fold, regime structure picked?" Use regime_at() want fixed regime tested across folds.

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
code: regime_spec(...)

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: ... kwargs passed verbatim detect_regime() spec invoked (e.g. loss, , min_run, method).

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: function one argument ("Triangle") returning "Regime" object. caller decides triangle pass (full vs. masked); inside backtest() always masked training triangle.

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Capture detection arguments, defer execution until fit time. spec <- regime_spec(loss = "loss_ata")  # In fit_ratio(): closure is invoked on the user's `tri`. fit <- fit_ratio(tri, loss_regime = regime_spec(loss = "loss_ata"))  # In backtest(): closure is invoked on the *masked* triangle of # each holdout fold, so detected change points never peek at # held-out cells. bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L,                loss_regime = regime_spec(loss = "loss_ata")) } # }

what: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
title: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
text: render() prints data frame, data table, Triangle object compact, fixed-width console table. layout deliberately mirrors dataframe console output package's Python sibling, lossratio-py, R Python implementations produce visually consistent table previews. rendered table four parts: shape: (rows, cols) header; box-drawn grid carrying column-name row column-type row (<int>, <dbl>, <date>, <chr>, ...); head / tail sample rows – object n rows middle collapsed single ellipsis row; "N variables" footer listing columns dropped table wide console. Columns selected ends inward fill width; dropped middle columns summarised footer. print.Triangle() delegates render(), Triangle objects print style default.

title: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
code: render(x, ...)  # Default S3 method render(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'data.table' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'tbl_df' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Triangle' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Triangle' print(x, ...)

title: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
text: x object render. Methods provided data frames, data tables, tibbles, Triangle objects; default method falls back print(). ... Additional arguments passed methods. n Integer; maximum number data rows show head / tail sampling applies. Default 10. width Integer; target console width characters. Default getOption("width", 100). max_col_width Integer; maximum width single column – longer values truncated ellipsis. Default 14. verbose Logical; whether print "variables" footer columns dropped fit width. Default TRUE.
