what: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: Check (groups, cohort) cohort consecutive dev sequence within observed range. Non-consecutive cohorts produce non-consecutive age--age links downstream (e.g., 14 -> 17 instead 14 -> 15), breaks summary.Link() key uniqueness causes cartesian joins fit_ratio(). function inspects raw data without modifying . Use as_triangle() decide whether fix data source, drop offending cohorts, pass fill_gaps = TRUE as_triangle(). Two checks performed: Cohort dev-sequence gaps – (group, cohort), report missing dev values within observed range. Row-level calendar consistency – calendar supplied, report rows calendar < cohort. rows logically impossible (claims precede policy issue) downstream show negative dev_m, polluting cohort dev sequences.

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
code: validate_triangle(   df,   groups = NULL,   cohort,   calendar = NULL,   dev = NULL,   grain = "auto" )

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: df data.frame. groups Grouping variable(s). cohort single cohort variable (raw column name). calendar Optional calendar period variable row-level consistency check. supplied, rows calendar < cohort flagged invalid. Default NULL (skip check). dev single development-period variable (raw column name). Optional calendar supplied – dev derived (cohort, calendar) resolved grain (dispatch as_triangle()). grain Grain string ("M" / "Q" / "H" / "Y") "auto" (default) – used dev derived (cohort, calendar). Ignored dev supplied.

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: data.table class "TriangleValidation" one row per cohort containing gaps. Columns: groups, cohort Cohort identifier. n_dev Number distinct observed dev values. n_expected max(dev) - min(dev) + 1 cohort. missing List column missing dev values. Returns zero-row data.table gaps found. Row-level violations (calendar supplied check finds ) attached "invalid_rows" attribute – data.table columns [groups, cohort, calendar, dev (present), reason]. Use attr(, "invalid_rows") rely print.TriangleValidation displays sections.

title: lossratio (development version)
text: Worker layer fix + bootstrap arg fit_cl / fit_ed. fit_bf() / fit_cc() / fit_sa() now build internal exposure fit calling fit_cl(loss = "exposure", ...) directly instead routing fit_exposure() — downward-Tier 3 -> Tier 4 dependency. fit_cl() fit_ed() gain bootstrap, B, seed, conf_level arguments symmetry SA / BF / CC workers; bootstrap = NULL (default) preserves analytical Mack SE. fit-result classes standardised c("XFit", "list") (prepended forms dispatchers). fit_bf() + fit_cc() promoted peer workers bootstrap composition. fit_bf takes external prior ELR (Bornhuetter-Ferguson 1972); fit_cc derives ELR data via payout weighting (Stanard 1985, Cape Cod). expose bootstrap = "auto" cell / link / parametric simulation analytical fallback. Promotion makes available fit_loss(method = "bf" | "cc"). fit_sa worker + fit_loss true dispatcher. Phase 4 split stage-adaptive composition (R/sa.R, class "SAFit") R/loss.R, fit_loss() now thin-dispatches method worker functions (fit_ed / fit_cl / fit_sa / fit_bf / fit_cc) augments output LossFit-uniform $full schema via .lossfit_augment(). fit_exposure() follows pattern .exposurefit_augment() + .exposurefit_bootstrap() (Phase 4c). BREAKING: bootstrap type = "parametric" -> "analytical" rename. Mack closed-form SE option previously mislabelled "parametric". textbook-parametric kernels (cell-distribution sampling + refit, England-Verrall 1999) now use type = "parametric", analytical Mack closed-form lives type = "analytical". bootstrap.Triangle() default "analytical"; worker-side fit_sa / fit_bf / fit_cc type = defaults "parametric" (cell-distribution simulation). SA nonparametric bootstrap proper kernel. bootstrap(method = "sa") previously silently dispatched CL cell kernel. Phase 1 introduced dedicated bootstrap_kernel_sa_cell (link variants) respect stage transition maturity k^*, ED-stage cells using additive g_k refit CL-stage cells using multiplicative f_k refit. ED bootstrap (Phase 1, fixed exposure). bootstrap() now supports method = "ed" residual = "cell": per-replicate g*_k refit additive forward projection (Delta loss = g_k * P_{} + noise) instead multiplicative chain ladder. Exposure stays fixed across replicates (projected via CL exposure column). New native helpers bootstrap_refit_gstar / bootstrap_fwd_proj_ed_and_clip / bootstrap_fwd_sim_ed_cell parallel CL kernel triple; C entry point C_bootstrap_kernel_ed_cell (17 args). Phase 2 / 3 (joint loss + exposure bootstrap) deferred. method = "ed" requires residual = "cell"; ED + link residuals implemented. bootstrap() method-enum reorder — c("sa", "cl", "ed") -> c("ed", "cl", "sa"). Matches fit_loss() / fit_ratio() default flip. Default now "ed". Users relying "sa" bootstrap method must pass explicitly. BREAKING: method default flip — "sa" -> "ed". fit_loss(), fit_ratio(), backtest() (via loss_method) now default method = "ed" (exposure-driven) instead "sa" (stage-adaptive). Method-enum order c("ed", "cl", "sa") — simple -> classical -> composition. Rationale: ED unconditional safe baseline (additive, maturity-detection dependency, robust early-dev age--age volatility); CL classical Mack 1993 alternative; SA composition ED + CL requiring 2-pass maturity detection. Users relying stage-adaptive behaviour must now pass method = "sa" explicitly. Migration: fit_loss(tri) -> still works (now defaults ED) fit_loss(tri, method = "sa") -> explicit (change) Want previous SA default behaviour back? -> add method = "sa" Variance helper rename — .mack_g_var -> .ed_g_var. Internal factor-level variance helper renamed paradigm clarity. .mack_* reserved CL/Mack 1993 paradigm (f-factor variance); ED intensity variance follows Buehlmann-Straub 1970 lineage now lives .ed_g_var(). two natural analytical variance helpers package now: .mack_f_var() (CL paradigm, f) .ed_g_var() (ED paradigm, g). Cross-paradigm pairs provided separate functions — algebraically derivable via g_k = f_k - 1. helpers now carry @references blocks citing Mack (1993) Buehlmann-Straub (1970) respectively. BREAKING: worker-arg rename target -> loss. Worker-layer functions (fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_ata, fit_intensity, detect_maturity, detect_regime) as_link() now take loss argument place target. Worker-output columns rename accordingly (target_obs -> loss_obs, target_proj -> loss_proj, target_*_se -> loss_*_se, target_from / target_to / target_delta -> loss_from / loss_to / loss_delta). Fit-object attribute key attr(., "target") becomes attr(., "loss") Link, ATAFit, EDFit, CLFit, IntensityFit, Maturity, Regime, Convergence objects. target arg backtest() (dispatcher enum selecting "ratio" / "loss" / "exposure") unchanged — different semantic (metric backtest) stays -. Bootstrap’s target = c("loss", "exposure") enum also unchanged. Migration: fit_cl(tri, target = "loss") -> fit_cl(tri, loss = "loss") fit_ed(tri, target = "loss", exposure = ...) -> fit_ed(tri, loss = "loss", exposure = ...) as_link(tri, target = "loss") -> as_link(tri, loss = "loss") detect_maturity(tri, target = "loss") -> detect_maturity(tri, loss = "loss") detect_regime(tri, target = "ratio") -> detect_regime(tri, loss = "ratio") Reading cl_fit$full$target_proj -> cl_fit$full$loss_proj Reading attr(link, "target") -> attr(link, "loss") BREAKING: identifier rename prem -> exposure, lr -> ratio. Framework-generic naming denominator slot (exposure) derived ratio column / fit family (ratio). previous -progress sweep premium -> prem (still unreleased) superseded — final target exposure, framework-generic word covers loss reserving (risk premium = exposure), frequency (insureds = exposure), severity (claim count = Bühlmann natural weight = exposure) uniformly. Prose noun phrases (“loss ratio”, “premium”, “risk premium”, “exposure measure”) unchanged. Migration (find--replace call sites): as_triangle(..., premium = "incr_prem") / as_triangle(..., prem = "incr_prem") -> as_triangle(..., exposure = "incr_exposure") as_triangle(..., development = "dev_m") -> as_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") validate_triangle(..., development = "dev_m") -> validate_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") fit_premium(...) / fit_prem(...) -> fit_exposure(...) fit_lr(...) -> fit_ratio(...) fit_loss(..., prem_method = ..., prem_alpha = ..., prem_fit = ...) -> fit_loss(..., exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ..., exposure_fit = ...) fit_lr(..., prem_method = ..., prem_alpha = ..., prem_regime = ...) -> fit_ratio(..., exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ..., exposure_regime = ...) backtest(..., target = "lr", prem_method = ..., prem_alpha = ...) -> backtest(..., target = "ratio", exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ...) backtest(..., target = "prem") -> backtest(..., target = "exposure") bootstrap(tri, target = "prem") -> bootstrap(tri, target = "exposure") LRFit$prem_alpha, LRFit$prem_regime -> RatioFit$exposure_alpha, $exposure_regime LossFit$prem_fit -> $exposure_fit S3 class "PremFit" -> "ExposureFit" (incl. print.PremFit() / summary.PremFit() -> print.ExposureFit() / summary.ExposureFit()) S3 class "LRFit" -> "RatioFit" (incl. print.LRFit() / summary.LRFit() / plot.LRFit() / plot_triangle.LRFit() -> *.RatioFit()) attr(PremFit_obj, "prem_method") -> attr(ExposureFit_obj, "exposure_method") Triangle / Calendar / Total columns: prem, incr_prem, prem_share, incr_prem_share, lr, incr_lr -> exposure, incr_exposure, exposure_share, incr_exposure_share, ratio, incr_ratio Fit output columns: prem_proj, prem_obs, prem_proc_se, prem_param_se, prem_total_se, prem_total_cv, prem_ci_lo, prem_ci_hi, incr_prem_proj -> exposure_*; lr_proj, lr_se, lr_cv, lr_ci_lo, lr_ci_hi, lr_ult, lr_latest, incr_lr_proj -> ratio_* R source files: R/prem.R, R/lr.R, R/lr-vis.R -> R/exposure.R, R/ratio.R, R/ratio-vis.R Raw dataset (data/experience.rda): column incr_prem -> incr_exposure (regenerated) package name lossratio unchanged; sweep purely code-identifier refactor. conceptual “loss ratio” framework preserved — ratio column / fit name loss--exposure ratio, package continues specialise long-term health insurance reserving developing exposure (risk premium) triangles. Default flip — bootstrap()’s keep_pseudo default changes TRUE FALSE. long-format $pseudo_triangles long-format data.table longer built every call; precomputed $summary (Pythagorean SE decomposition + optional percentile CI) still always present. Skipping long-format reshape saves roughly 250-300 ms ~200 MB typical 4-group monthly triangle B = 999. Users inspect $pseudo_triangles directly pass keep_pseudo = TRUE explicitly; argument unchanged. BREAKING — four constructor functions renamed build_* as_* align tidyverse coercion idiom Python sibling’s lr.Triangle(df) mental model: build_triangle() -> as_triangle() build_calendar() -> as_calendar() build_total() -> as_total() build_link() -> as_link() signature change, verb. Migration global find--replace. functions still validate, coerce, aggregate substantively – as_* name reflects returned object canonical lossratio shape derived raw experience data, just thin type cast. PascalCase classes (Triangle, Calendar, Total, Link) remain unchanged. BREAKING — plot_triangle.Triangle() argument type renamed view parity plot_triangle.CLFit(), plot_triangle.LRFit(), plot_triangle.Backtest(), already used view = c("value", "usage"). type = slot left free plot-method-specific semantics (plot.Backtest(type = "col"/"diag"/"cell"), plot.CLFit(type = "projection"/"reserve"), etc.). Migration: plot_triangle(tri, type = "usage") -> plot_triangle(tri, view = "usage"). Known limitation (future work) — segment_wise mini-triangle gap-fill. treatment = "segment_wise", fit_* produces mini-triangle per regime segment using segment’s cohorts; cells fall outside every mini-triangle (typically old cohorts late development periods reached single segment) currently left unprojected. follow-phase add fallback (e.g. latest-segment factor, previous-segment factor, smoothing across segments) controlled Regime$fallback knob, plus warning k*k^* exceeds change--now horizon latest segment (also cause mini-triangle truncated). Today cells render unused plot_triangle(view = "usage") rather filled inferred projection. BREAKING — backtest() cell-level columns renamed target_actual / target_proj (_incr siblings) actual / expected (actual_incr / expected_incr). new names match actuarial /E convention (aeg = actual - expected, ae_err = actual / expected - 1) self-document role column. Worker-layer column names (target_proj etc. CLFit$full / EDFit$full) unchanged – rename scoped backtest$ae_err. Migration: replace bt$ae_err$target_actual bt$ae_err$actual, bt$ae_err$target_proj bt$ae_err$expected. backtest() result slot fit_fn_name renamed dispatcher clarity (value still dispatcher name — fit_ratio / fit_loss / fit_exposure — selected target=). print() / summary() labels updated accordingly. fit_loss(), fit_exposure(), fit_ratio(), backtest() now attach $usage data.table result: one row per (group, cohort, dev) cell pre-filter triangle status factor (used / unused / holdout / future). plot_triangle(fit, view = "usage") reads directly instead re-deriving filter logic plot time, heatmap always matches cells fit actually saw. New internal helper .build_usage() packages 2-pass maturity detection plus .compute_triangle_usage() attaches filter metadata (regime / recent / holdout / m_k / m_k_dt) data.table attributes renderer. BREAKING — build_triangle(), build_total(), validate_triangle() rename dev = argument development =. new name explicit development-period axis (matching coh <- cohort symmetry inside function bodies). Migration: replace build_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") build_triangle(..., development = "dev_m"). BREAKING — backtest() result columns renamed value_proj / value_actual (_incr variants) target_proj / target_actual (_incr variants), matching worker-layer target_* generic convention. BREAKING — summary column renames <metric>_<stat> consistency: summary.LossFit / summary.PremiumFit: se_ultimate / cv_ultimate -> ultimate_se / ultimate_cv. .compute_dv() output: median_lr / mad_lr -> lr_median / lr_mad. Internal var_lr (LR variance scratch column) -> lr_var. detect_regime_optimal_window() diagnostics column: mean_magnitude -> magnitude_mean. plot_triangle() now derives axis grain via attr(tri, "grain") raw column name (uy_m, cy_q, …) one package-standard forms, user-supplied names like uym elap_m still render tick labels abbreviated format (23.04, 23.1Q, …). plot_triangle(fit, view = "usage") regime / segment_wise routing fixed: per-group dispatch now honours regime$groups even multi_group = FALSE, regime_at(coverage = "SUR", ...) scopes hline / cohort cut SUR facet . Filtered-cells render unused (gray) instead future (white). .datatable.aware <- TRUE declared R/zzz.R; data.table NSE NOTEs suppressed via mlr3-style (".col") := LHS pattern + reorganised globalVariables() list. Internal temp markers (.col prefix) use function-local NULL bindings per data.table official recommendation. BREAKING — as_experience(), check_experience(), is_experience() removed along Experience S3 class. build_triangle() already validates required columns, coerces dates numerics, aggregates inline, explicit coercion step longer needed (class never required downstream function). Migration: replace exp <- as_experience(df); build_triangle(exp, ...) build_triangle(df, ...). Matches Python sibling 0.0.1.dev7. New fit_intensity() + IntensityFit S3 class (R/intensity.R) — factor-level ED diagnostic, parallel fit_ata() multiplicative side. Returns per-link WLS-estimated intensities g_k standard errors diagnostic stats; projection. ED maturity concept, fit_intensity deliberately omits maturity_args. Link cell-level column g renamed intensity (concept-based, parallels ATA’s ata). Summary / fit per-link output columns (g, g_se, g_var, g_selected) keep Mack-style symbol naming parallelism ATA summary’s f, f_se. Layered naming: cell layer uses concept (intensity), summary layer uses symbol (g). backtest(): cell-level metric aggregation columns renamed aeg ae_err (column ae_err, aggregations ae_err_mean / ae_err_med / ae_err_wt). Print plot labels updated “/E Error”. Formula unchanged: (actual - pred) / pred.

title: lossratio 0.0.0.9000
text: Aggregation: build_triangle() (cohort × dev), build_calendar() (calendar period), build_total() (portfolio total). build_triangle() validates schema coerces required columns inline. Link table: build_link() returns Link object covering single-variable (ATA-style) dual-variable (ED-style) workflows. summary.Link(model = "ata"|"ed") dispatches matching diagnostic. Estimation: fit_ata() (per-link factors ); fit_ed(), fit_cl(), fit_lr() (factors + projection). fit_lr supports three methods — "sa" (default), "ed", "cl". Cell-selection diagnostics: detect_maturity() (dev axis — link beyond ATA factors stable), detect_regime() (cohort axis — structural breaks across underwriting cohorts). Projection diagnostic: detect_convergence() (operates fitted LRFit; valuation depth projected ultimate loss ratio stops revising). Backtest: backtest() (calendar-diagonal hold-, supports fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_lr). Visualisation: S3 plot() plot_triangle() methods every fit class.
